prompt: Design a crash-resilient agent run loop that can resume mid-task after a process crash. Describe the checkpoint format, what state is persisted, and how work is re-picked on restart.
Give 5 distinct approaches to bounding an infinite ingestion loop that reads from a feed forever without unbounded memory growth. For each, note the failure mode it prevents.
Optimistic file-locks versus a database for a multi-worker claim queue where parallel agents must claim tasks without duplicating work: which would you choose and why? State the decisive tradeoffs.
Explain the tradeoffs of re-running an oracle at scoring time instead of trusting agent-reported scores. When is the extra compute worth it, and what failure class does it close?

Implement a dead-end registry that stops a long-running agent from re-exploring directions that already failed. Describe the key, the store, and how a candidate direction is checked before work starts.
You must run untrusted model-generated code during an eval. Compare sandboxing options (subprocess limits, containers, WASM) and pick a default for a single-node research harness. Justify the choice.
Propose ways to search over experiment configurations as a tree with checkpoints so promising branches can be resumed and dead branches pruned. Sketch the node state and the expansion policy.
Explain how to hand off a subtask from one agent to another agent so the receiver has enough context to continue, while restricting which tools the sub-agent may call.
Implement a debounce function in TypeScript. It should delay invoking the callback until the input has stopped firing for a given wait, and support a trailing call.
What is the cleanest way to center a div both horizontally and vertically with flexbox? Give the CSS and say why it is preferable to older techniques.
Write a haiku about the tide.
Explain HTTP status 429 and list several client-side strategies for handling it gracefully.
